binaries.title: intelide.sys - Uninitialized PCI-config stack buffers zero-initialized in init-only enumeration callbacks (CWE-908, not attacker-reachable)
binaries.note: The patched build is a full recompile; every function is relocated. The intentional source-level change is added zero-initialization (memset plus inlined stack-variable zeroing) in three PciIdeXInitialize enumeration callbacks. All affected routines run only during controller initialization/enumeration and read the controller's own PCI configuration space; they are not reachable from any untrusted IOCTL/user surface. Remaining differences are compiler codegen and CRT/toolchain additions.

changed_functions.name: PiixIdeGetControllerProperties
key_changes: Added memset (memset @ 0x1C0002200) to zero the 0x40-byte PCI config buffer (var_E8 unpatched / var_E0 patched) before the first PciIdeXGetBusData
Added memset to zero the 0x56-byte PCI config buffer (var_A8 unpatched / var_A0 patched) before the second PciIdeXGetBusData
Stack frame reorganization (lea rbp, [rsp-0x27]) and register reallocation to accommodate the memset calls
The read data is the IDE controller's own PCI configuration space (device ID / programming-interface classification); it is consumed only on a successful (>=0) PciIdeXGetBusData return
changed_functions.security_notes: Genuine CWE-908 zero-initialization hardening. The unpatched function passed these stack buffers to PciIdeXGetBusData without zeroing them; the patched build zeroes them first. Direction is correct (patched is stricter). Reachability: this is the GetControllerProperties callback registered with PciIdeXInitialize and invoked by the PCIIDEX port driver during controller enumeration, not from an untrusted IOCTL/user path. The buffer content is the controller's own PCI config space and is only used on success. Not attacker-reachable in the software threat model; severity informational.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (no zeroing before PciIdeXGetBusData):
  0x1C0001069: cmp     dword ptr [rdx], 50h
  0x1C0001072: jz      0x1C000107E
  0x1C000107E: mov     r9d, 40h
  0x1C0001084: lea     rdx, [rsp+118h+var_E8]   ; NOT zeroed
  0x1C0001089: xor     r8d, r8d
  0x1C000108C: call    cs:__imp_PciIdeXGetBusData
  ; second buffer:
  0x1C00012D3: mov     r9d, 56h
  0x1C00012D9: lea     rdx, [rsp+118h+var_A8]   ; NOT zeroed
  0x1C00012E1: mov     rcx, rbx
  0x1C00012E4: call    cs:__imp_PciIdeXGetBusData

PATCHED (buffers zeroed first):
  0x1C0001080: mov     esi, 40h
  0x1C0001085: lea     rcx, [rsp+110h+var_E0]
  0x1C000108A: mov     r8d, esi
  0x1C000108D: xor     edx, edx
  0x1C000108F: call    memset                   ; memset(&var_e0, 0, 0x40)
  0x1C0001094: cmp     dword ptr [rdi], 50h
  0x1C00010A3: mov     r9d, esi
  0x1C00010A6: lea     rdx, [rsp+110h+var_E0]    ; zeroed
  0x1C00010B1: call    cs:__imp_PciIdeXGetBusData
  ; second buffer:
  0x1C00012E2: mov     r14d, 56h
  0x1C00012E8: lea     rcx, [rbp+57h+var_A0]
  0x1C00012EC: mov     r8d, r14d
  0x1C00012EF: xor     edx, edx
  0x1C00012F1: call    memset                   ; memset(&var_a0, 0, 0x56)
  0x1C00012F9: lea     rdx, [rbp+57h+var_A0]     ; zeroed
  0x1C0001303: call    cs:__imp_PciIdeXGetBusData

changed_functions.name: PiixIdeChannelEnabled
key_changes: Added memset (memset @ 0x1C0002200) to zero a 0x4c-byte stack region (var_68) before the channel-status PciIdeXGetBusData
Prologue change (push rdi added, rbx spilled) to accommodate the memset call
The 4-byte read at PCI offset 0x40 populates [rsp+0x60]; the channel-enabled bit is byte [rsp+rbx*2+0x61] shifted right by 7
changed_functions.security_notes: Genuine CWE-908 zero-initialization hardening on the ChannelEnabled callback (registered at [arg2+0x18]). The unpatched build read 4 bytes of the controller's own PCI config space into an unzeroed stack region; the patched build zeroes 0x4c bytes first. The read result is only consumed on success. Init-only enumeration path; not attacker-reachable. Severity informational.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED:
  0x1C00015A6: mov     r9d, 4
  0x1C00015AC: lea     rdx, [rsp+88h+var_28]   ; NOT zeroed
  0x1C00015B1: lea     r8d, [r9+3Ch]           ; PCI offset 0x40
  0x1C00015B5: call    cs:__imp_PciIdeXGetBusData
  0x1C00015CC: movzx   eax, [rsp+rbx*2+88h+var_27]
  0x1C00015D1: shr     eax, 7

PATCHED:
  0x1C00015D1: xor     edx, edx
  0x1C00015D3: lea     rcx, [rsp+88h+var_68]
  0x1C00015D8: lea     r8d, [rdx+4Ch]
  0x1C00015DC: call    memset                  ; memset(&var_68, 0, 0x4c)
  0x1C00015F3: lea     rdx, [rsp+88h+var_28]    ; zeroed
  0x1C00015FF: call    cs:__imp_PciIdeXGetBusData
  0x1C0001616: movzx   eax, [rsp+rbx*2+88h+var_27]

changed_functions.name: PiixIdeTransferModeSelect
key_changes: Zero-initializes five timing-output stack locals at function entry (var_18/word, arg_10/byte, arg_18/byte, var_20/byte, var_14/word) via 'xor esi,esi' plus a series of stores
Register reallocation to free esi for the zeroing sequence
These locals are passed by reference to PiixIdepTransferModeSelect and later used as data buffers for PciIdeXSetBusData writes to PCI config offsets 0x44/0x48/0x4a/0x54
changed_functions.security_notes: CWE-908 zero-initialization added, but redundant: PiixIdepTransferModeSelect writes all six of its output parameters unconditionally at its tail before its single 'xor eax,eax; return 0' path, so the locals are never consumed uninitialized even in the unpatched build. The earlier claim that the callee has code paths leaving outputs unwritten is not supported by the disassembly. Init-only TransferModeSelect callback (registered at [arg2+0x28]); not attacker-reachable. Severity informational.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (no local zeroing):
  0x1C0001673: sub     rsp, 60h
  0x1C0001677: mov     r14d, 2
  0x1C000172E: call    PiixIdepTransferModeSelect   ; writes all outputs before returning 0
  ; outputs later used as SetBusData buffers at 0x1C0001761/17AA/18E8/191D/1978

PATCHED (locals zeroed at entry):
  0x1C00016C3: sub     rsp, 60h
  0x1C00016C7: xor     esi, esi
  0x1C00016D0: mov     [rbp+var_18], si
  0x1C00016D7: mov     [rbp+arg_10], sil
  0x1C00016DE: mov     [rbp+arg_18], sil
  0x1C00016E6: mov     [rbp+var_20], sil
  0x1C00016ED: mov     [rbp+var_14], si
  0x1C0001793: call    PiixIdepTransferModeSelect

changed_functions.name: PiixIdepTransferModeSelect
key_changes: Control-flow restructuring (compound conditions vs goto-label patterns) and register reallocation
Logic preserved; the function writes all six output parameters unconditionally at its tail before its single 'return 0' path
changed_functions.security_notes: Compiler codegen/register-allocation differences with preserved behavior. Relevant to Finding on PiixIdeTransferModeSelect: this callee always defines all outputs before returning success, which is why the caller's added zero-init is redundant.

changed_functions.name: PiixIdeUdmaModesSupported
key_changes: Minor loop-shape codegen difference in the trailing-zero counting loop for the second UDMA mode byte
Behavior verified identical across representative inputs
changed_functions.security_notes: Compiler codegen difference only; not listed as security-relevant. Included for completeness of the independent diff.

changed_functions.name: __GSHandlerCheckCommon
key_changes: Byte load changed from 'mov dl, byte [rcx+rax+3]' (0x1C0002041) to 'movzx edx, byte [rcx+rax+3]' (0x1C0002091)
Subsequent use changed from 'movzx eax, dl' to 'mov eax, edx'
changed_functions.security_notes: Compiler-generated /GS stack-cookie handler helper from the C runtime. Both sequences compute the identical masked value; the unpatched path fully consumes the byte via 'movzx eax, dl', so there is no stale-bit information leak and no behavioral difference. MSVC codegen churn from the recompile, not a security fix. The earlier CWE-197 'partial register write' characterization was unfounded.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED __GSHandlerCheckCommon @ 0x1C0002004:
  0x1C0002041: mov     dl, [rcx+rax+3]
  0x1C0002045: test    dl, 0Fh
  0x1C000204A: movzx   eax, dl
  0x1C000204D: and     eax, 0FFFFFFF0h

PATCHED __GSHandlerCheckCommon @ 0x1C0002054:
  0x1C0002091: movzx   edx, byte ptr [rcx+rax+3]
  0x1C0002096: test    dl, 0Fh
  0x1C000209B: mov     eax, edx
  0x1C000209D: and     eax, 0FFFFFFF0h

added: memset @ 0x1C0002200 (CRT, required by the added zero-init calls)

security_findings.entry_point: PciIdeXInitialize GetControllerProperties callback, invoked by the PCIIDEX port driver during controller enumeration
call_chain: PciIdeXGetBusData (reads controller's own PCI config space into a stack buffer)
security_findings.description: The unpatched function passes a 0x40-byte and a 0x56-byte stack buffer to PciIdeXGetBusData without zeroing them. The patched build adds memset before each read. This is genuine CWE-908 hardening with the correct direction (patched stricter). The buffers hold the IDE controller's own PCI configuration space, read during enumeration, and are consumed only on a successful (>=0) return. The zeroing guards the theoretical 'success with short read' case. The function is an init-only enumeration callback, not reachable from any untrusted IOCTL/user path, so it is not attacker-reachable in the software threat model.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: The only conceivable influence on buffer contents requires control of the physical/emulated IDE controller hardware whose PCI config space is read (a hardware threat model). The read sizes (0x40, 0x56) and offset (0) are compile-time constants, not caller-controlled.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. Worst realistic effect of the unpatched state, and only under an actual short read, is mis-classification or mis-timing of the local IDE controller (robustness/DoS on attacker-controlled hardware), not memory corruption of unrelated kernel state.
security_findings.evidence: See assembly_diff for PiixIdeGetControllerProperties in changed_functions.
security_findings.assembly: See assembly_diff for PiixIdeGetControllerProperties in changed_functions.

security_findings.entry_point: PciIdeX TransferModeSelect callback (registered at [arg2+0x28] by PiixIdeGetControllerProperties), invoked by the PCIIDEX framework during controller configuration
call_chain: PciIdeXSetBusData writes to PCI config offsets 0x44/0x48/0x4a/0x54
security_findings.description: The unpatched function declares five stack locals passed by reference to PiixIdepTransferModeSelect and later used as PciIdeXSetBusData data buffers. The patched build zero-initializes them at entry. This zeroing is redundant: PiixIdepTransferModeSelect writes all six output parameters unconditionally at its tail before its single 'return 0' path, so the locals are never consumed uninitialized even in the unpatched build. Init-only configuration callback; not attacker-reachable.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Runs during IDE transfer-mode configuration under the PCIIDEX framework. No untrusted IOCTL/user input reaches this path; the SetBusData offsets and sizes are compile-time constants.
security_findings.primitive: None. Because the callee always defines the outputs, no uninitialized value flows to PciIdeXSetBusData.
security_findings.evidence: See assembly_diff for PiixIdeTransferModeSelect and the security_notes for PiixIdepTransferModeSelect in changed_functions.
security_findings.assembly: See assembly_diff for PiixIdeTransferModeSelect in changed_functions.

security_findings.entry_point: PciIdeX ChannelEnabled callback (registered at [arg2+0x18] by PiixIdeGetControllerProperties)
call_chain: PciIdeXGetBusData reads 4 bytes of the controller's own PCI config space
security_findings.description: The unpatched function reads 4 bytes at PCI offset 0x40 into an unzeroed stack region and then reads byte [rsp+rbx*2+0x61] (>>7) for the channel-enabled state. The patched build zeroes a 0x4c-byte region first. The read result is only consumed on success. Genuine CWE-908 hardening; init-only enumeration path; not attacker-reachable.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Called during IDE channel enumeration. Channel index arg2 is constrained to 0 or 1 ('test edx, 0xfffffffe'). Read size (4) and offset (0x40) are compile-time constants.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. At most an incorrect channel-enabled report on attacker-controlled hardware under a short read.
security_findings.evidence: See assembly_diff for PiixIdeChannelEnabled in changed_functions.
security_findings.assembly: See assembly_diff for PiixIdeChannelEnabled in changed_functions.

security_findings.entry_point: Compiler-generated /GS stack-cookie handler helper (C runtime), invoked during structured exception handling of stack-protected functions
security_findings.description: The byte load changed from 'mov dl, byte [rcx+rax+3]' with a later 'movzx eax, dl' to 'movzx edx, byte [rcx+rax+3]' with a later 'mov eax, edx'. Both compute the identical masked value and the unpatched path fully consumes the byte via movzx, so there is no stale-bit information leak and no behavioral difference. This is MSVC codegen churn from the recompile, not a security fix. The earlier CWE-197 'partial register write' claim was unfounded.

summary: The patch is a full recompile whose only intentional source-level security-relevant change is the addition of zero-initialization (a CRT memset plus inlined stack-variable zeroing) before PciIdeXGetBusData/PciIdeXSetBusData in three PciIdeXInitialize enumeration callbacks: PiixIdeGetControllerProperties (0x1C0001040 -> 0x1C0001050), PiixIdeChannelEnabled (0x1C0001580 -> 0x1C00015B0), and PiixIdeTransferModeSelect (0x1C0001660 -> 0x1C00016B0). These are genuine CWE-908 hardening changes with the correct direction (patched is stricter), but all affected routines run only during controller initialization/enumeration and read the IDE controller's own PCI configuration space; none is reachable from an untrusted IOCTL/user surface, so this is defense-in-depth rather than remediation of an attacker-reachable vulnerability. For PiixIdeTransferModeSelect the zeroing is additionally redundant because the timing calculator PiixIdepTransferModeSelect writes all its output parameters before returning. The __GSHandlerCheckCommon difference (0x1C0002004 -> 0x1C0002054) is a compiler codegen change with no security relevance. The patched-only functions (memset family, __cpu_features_init, XFG stub) are CRT/toolchain support introduced by the recompile. No omitted security-relevant change was found.
